summary: Completed full backend + frontend testing of Find Your Funny comedy event listing app. All 27 pytest backend cases pass (auth register/login/me/logout with blacklisting, events CRUD with organizer auth, category/city filters, nearby-public with distance sort, authenticated nearby, signups + double-signup rejection + organizer-only signup list + cancel, me/created, me/signedup, reverse geocode). All critical UI flows work: home, register → auto-login, login/logout state in navbar, create event with redirect to detail page, organizer sees edit/delete + signup list, non-organizer signup → cancel toggles correctly, category tabs on /events, profile created/signed tabs, Near Me gracefully handles geolocation denial. No critical or minor bugs found.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~612 lines; consider splitting by domain (auth, events, signups, geo) into modules once features grow beyond ~800 lines.
Register endpoint stores lowercased email but uniqueness check happens after that — fine, but also consider normalizing username case too (currently case-sensitive unique index).

CORS uses allow_credentials=True with allow_origins='*' from default env (backend/.env has CORS_ORIGINS="*"). Browsers will reject credentials with wildcard origins. Since the frontend uses Bearer tokens in localStorage (not cookies), this is OK for current auth, but update CORS_ORIGINS to explicit origins if cookies are ever adopted.
geocode_address and reverse_geocode use synchronous `requests` inside async endpoints — they block the event loop. Switch to httpx.AsyncClient or run in threadpool for better concurrency.

Token blacklist index on 'revokedAt' won't help revocation lookups; lookup query uses 'jti' — add an index on `jti` (unique). Consider TTL index on revokedAt to auto-expire entries after JWT_EXP_HOURS.
events/nearby silently filters out events with null lat/lon (due to geocode rate-limit). Consider returning a flag/warning so the UI can surface 'some events hidden'.

Event update: if organizer changes address, latitude/longitude is only updated when geocode succeeds; on failure stale coords are kept. Could clear them to null on failed re-geocode or surface a warning.
No rate limiting or brute-force lockout on /auth/login — add (e.g., 5 fails / 15 min) for production.
test_credentials: Pre-existing user micdrop/secret123 (per handoff); tests create their own users dynamically with TEST_-prefixed usernames.
seed_data_creation: Tests create ephemeral users and one event per run, and delete the event at the end. A few TEST_ users remain in users collection — harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py is a complete regression; rerun with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py -v. Playwright UI flows validated: register/login/logout, create event → detail → signup/cancel, profile tabs, category filter, near-me geolocation denial path. If retesting after code changes, also verify the code review comments above (especially sync requests in async handlers).
